expectations.text: References or applies the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle (or test-driven development) for skill authoring
expectations.evidence: Describes a baseline-test-then-write process but never names it RED-GREEN-REFACTOR, TDD, or test-driven development.

expectations.text: Mentions pressure scenarios, failure modes, or edge cases as the starting point for skill design
expectations.evidence: Final paragraph: 'run a baseline test. Give the draft to an agent... Document where it guesses, where it fails, where it rationalizes exceptions. Then write the skill to address those specific failures.'

expectations.text: Flags that the skill lacks failure modes, pressure scenarios, or edge cases
expectations.evidence: States 'No Common Mistakes section' and asks 'What goes wrong when people try these techniques?' with specific examples like check-ins running 20 minutes and action items with no owner.

expectations.text: Points out that there are no test cases or verification criteria to know if the skill actually works
expectations.evidence: Final section: 'Before rewriting, follow the skill creation process: run a baseline test. Give the draft to an agent... and watch what happens. Document where it guesses, where it fails.'
